Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: Outlines the topics covered in the NumPy module, including array creation, element-wise operations, universal functions, and broadcasting, serving as a syllabus-style reference for learners and instructors navigating the module content.

Artifacts.Name: NumPy_for_Engineering
Artifacts.Description: An interactive HTML lesson on NumPy for engineering that guides learners through ndarray basics, array creation, vectorized operations, broadcasting, indexing, linear algebra, and practical engineering applications with explanations and embedded code examples.

Artifacts.Name: Assignment 5 Open Channel derived
Artifacts.Description: A derived version of Assignment 5 that directs students to use NumPy library functions and the Newton-Raphson iterative method specifically to numerically solve for the depth of flow in an open channel, submitted as a Colab notebook.

Artifacts.Name: Assignment 5 Open Channel
Artifacts.Description: The original Assignment 5 that instructs students to apply Manning's Equation and an iterative numerical technique to calculate the depth of flow in an open channel, submitted as a Colab notebook.

Artifacts.Name: Assignment 5 – Find Depth of Flow in an Open Channel
Artifacts.Description: An instructor-authored summary page for Assignment 5 describing the task of solving Manning's Equation for flow depth in a trapezoidal open channel with a 50 cfs target flowrate using Python functions with NumPy or math libraries and iterative methods.

Artifacts.Name: NumPy_for_Engineering
Artifacts.Description: An interactive HTML lesson on NumPy for engineering that guides learners through ndarray basics, array creation, vectorized operations, broadcasting, indexing, linear algebra, and practical engineering applications with explanations and embedded code examples.

Artifacts.Name: External Materials Index
Artifacts.Description: An index page listing the external materials for the NumPy module, currently containing one item — Assignment 5 on finding depth of flow in an open channel — giving learners and instructors a quick reference to supplementary resources.
